## Why Choose BuyAndScrap for Cheap Cars in Plymouth?

When you're after **cheap cars Plymouth**, you want peace of mind, not hidden surprises. Here’s why BuyAndScrap should be your first port of call:

### Honest Listings, Real Value

Our platform is built on trust. Sellers are encouraged to provide clear, honest descriptions and plenty of photos, so you know exactly what you're looking at before you even pick up the phone. No fluffy sales talk, just real cars from real people, often at prices that beat the big dealers.

### All Cars with MOT

Supporting local is always a good thing, isn't it? Our listings feature cars from individuals and small traders right here in Plymouth and the surrounding areas. This often means easier viewings, quicker transactions, and the chance to buy from someone who knows the car and the local roads.

## What to Look For When Buying Cheap Cars in Plymouth

Even when you're buying a budget car, a bit of savvy goes a long way. Here are our top tips for bagging a bargain that won't let you down:

### Check the MOT History

Since all our cars have an MOT, take advantage of it! Use the government's free online MOT history checker with the car's registration number. It’ll show you past advisories and failures, giving you a better idea of the car’s maintenance history and potential recurring issues. It’s a goldmine of information!

### Service History is Key

Even if it’s just a stack of receipts, evidence of regular servicing (oil changes, brake checks, etc.) suggests the previous owner cared for the car. A well-maintained cheap car will always outlast one that’s been neglected.

### A Thorough Test Drive is Essential

Never buy a car without taking it for a spin. Listen for strange noises, feel for vibrations, check the brakes, clutch, and steering. Drive it at various speeds and on different road types if possible. Don't be shy – it's your money!

### Inspect for Common Issues

Do a quick visual check. Look for rust around the arches, sills, and underneath. Check tyre treads, lights, indicators, and warning lights on the dashboard. A little bit of wear and tear is expected on cheap cars, but you're looking for anything that screams "major repair needed soon!".
